# Image Attributions (Open-License Assets)

## Ada Lovelace Portrait

- File: `assets/images/open-license/ada-lovelace-portrait.jpg`
- Source page: <https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Ada_Lovelace_portrait.jpg>
- Direct asset URL: <https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/a/a4/Ada_Lovelace_portrait.jpg>
- Creator: Public domain historical portrait (Wikimedia Commons metadata)
- License: Public domain
- Notes: Used in Chapter 1 as an example open-license image.
## Gutenberg Bible (Lenox Copy, NYPL)

- File: `assets/images/wikimedia/gutenberg-bible.jpg`
- Source page: <https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Gutenberg_Bible,_Lenox_Copy,_New_York_Public_Library,_2009._Pic_01.jpg>
- Creator: Photographed by NYC Wanderer (Kevin Eng) / New York Public Library
- License: CC BY-SA 2.0
- Notes: Used in Chapter 1, Section 3 (printing press).

## Plato Bust (Silanion copy, Musei Capitolini MC1377)

- File: `assets/images/wikimedia/plato-bust.jpg`
- Source page: <https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Plato_Silanion_Musei_Capitolini_MC1377.jpg>
- Creator: Marie-Lan Nguyen (Wikimedia Commons)
- License: Public domain (ancient sculpture, modern photograph CC0)
- Notes: Used in Chapter 1, Section 2 (Plato and Socrates on writing).

## John Stuart Mill Portrait (c. 1870)

- File: `assets/images/wikimedia/john-stuart-mill.jpg`
- Source page: <https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:John_Stuart_Mill_by_London_Stereoscopic_Company,_c1870.jpg>
- Creator: London Stereoscopic Company
- License: Public domain (photograph from c. 1870, well past copyright term)
- Notes: Used in Chapter 1, Section 4 (Mill and the telegraph).

## Hannah Arendt Portrait (1933)

- File: `assets/images/wikimedia/hannah-arendt.jpg`
- Source page: <https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Hannah_Arendt_1933.jpg>
- Creator: Unknown photographer, 1933
- License: Public domain (published before 1928)
- Notes: Used in Chapter 1, Section 5 (Arendt and propaganda).
## Reuse Guidance

- Verify each image’s license before adding it to this folder.
- Keep source page and license text/summary with each entry.
- Prefer Wikimedia Commons assets with clear license metadata.
